channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Customs and Laws Still Lock Kenyan Women Out of Land Ownership
item.description: In Kenya, land ownership remains a major symbol of power and identity, yet many women are still denied their fair share due to deep-rooted traditions and gender norms. The article explores how, despit...

item.title: Tanzanian Churches Use Good Friday to Preach Forgiveness, Humility, and Justice
item.description: Across Tanzania, Christians came together on Good Friday to reflect on the suffering and crucifixion of Jesus Christ, marking the day with deep messages of forgiveness, humility, and peace. From Arush...

item.title: US military rescues airman after Iran downs fighter jet amid ongoing conflict
item.description: The United States successfully rescued an airman who was trapped behind enemy lines after Iran shot down his F-15 fighter jet. This operation is seen as a major achievement for the U.S. in the sixth w...

item.title: US Rescues Airman from Iran Amid Strait of Hormuz Standoff
item.description: US forces successfully rescued a wounded airman whose F-15 jet was shot down over Iran, after he evaded capture for over a day in rugged mountains using only a pistol, communicator, and beacon. Presid...

item.title: NDLEA Dismantles Drug Syndicate Hiding Cocaine in Palm Kernel Oil Shipments to the UK
item.description: NDLEA officials don burst one serious international drug trafficking ring wey dey use tins of palm kernel oil take hide cocaine wey dem wan export go United Kingdom. The operation na result of three w...

item.title: The Royal Family attends Easter Sunday service at Windsor amid family absences and ongoing controversies
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla attended the traditional Easter Sunday service at St George’s Chapel in Windsor, joined by several members of the Royal Family. The Prince and Princess of Wales, Wil...

item.title: State pensions to rise by £575 per year from tomorrow, affecting over 12 million people
item.description: From tomorrow, over 12 million people in the United Kingdom will see their state pensions increase by up to £575 annually. This rise is a result of the government’s commitment to the 'triple lock' gua...

item.title: Two families displaced after tree falls on homes during Storm Dave
item.description: In Staffordshire, two families have been rendered homeless after a large tree toppled onto their homes during the heavy winds of Storm Dave. The incident occurred on Windsor Crescent, Kniver, at appro...

item.title: Pope Leo urges peace and resilience during his first Easter Mass
item.description: In Vatican City, Pope Leo celebrated his first Easter Mass as pontiff, delivering a heartfelt appeal for hope and peace in a world beset by violent conflicts. Addressing thousands gathered in St. Pete...

item.title: EPRA Appoints Joseph Oketch as Acting Director General Following Daniel Kiptoo’s Resignation Over Substandard Fuel Scandal
item.description: The Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA) has appointed Joseph Oketch as its acting Director General after the resignation of Daniel Kiptoo, who stepped down amid a major fuel scandal invol...

item.title: Quebec and Alberta Explore Autonomy and Interprovincial Influence
item.description: Alberta and Quebec are increasingly asserting their provincial autonomy within Canada, each drawing lessons from the other's political strategies. Alberta Premier Danielle Smith has promised an Octobe...

item.title: SpaceX Explores Orbital Data Centers as Part of Its Growth Strategy
item.description: SpaceX has confidentially filed for an initial public offering (IPO) aiming to raise $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation. Central to CEO Elon Musk’s vision are orbital data centers, which could ...

item.title: Celebrating 75 Years of Dennis the Menace and the Timeless Appeal of Mischievous Children
item.description: For the past 75 years, Dennis the Menace, the iconic British comic character, has delighted children with his cheeky antics and mischievous nature. Debuting in the Beano comic in March 1951, Dennis is...

item.title: Lagos Hosts Pre-Launch of LuxuryByFeyi Fashion Showroom and Feyiartistery Art Gallery
item.description: Lagos recently dey groove small as LuxuryByFeyi Fashion Showroom and Feyiartistery Art Gallery run their pre-launch opening for Ikoyi. The fashion side show dem SS26 collection wey dem call 'Circular ...

item.title: Nigel Farage and Reform UK's Energy Bill Giveaway: Populism Meets Viral Politics
item.description: Kirsty Major’s opinion piece in The Guardian analyses Nigel Farage’s latest publicity stunt: an energy bill lottery launched by Reform UK under the slogan “Nigel cut my bills.” The initiative promises...

item.title: Reflecting on Ewart Grogan’s legacy and Kenya’s colonial past
item.description: This article from Nation.Africa is a creative reflection where the writer imagines having a talk with the ghost of Ewart Grogan, one of the most daring figures of early colonial Kenya. Grogan, known f...

item.title: Practical ways to manage and cope with rejection
item.description: Rejection is an unavoidable part of life and it can feel very painful, activating the same areas of the brain as physical pain. This article highlights that feeling rejected is normal and affects ever...

item.title: Pietermaritzburg High Court cancels unlawful subpoena against AGSA
item.description: The Pietermaritzburg High Court has struck down a subpoena that was issued against the Auditor-General of South Africa (AGSA), declaring it an unlawful abuse of court process. The case arose after Sol...

item.title: Pakistani Leaders Appreciate Christian Community’s Role in National Development on Easter
item.description: On the occasion of Easter, President Asif Ali Zardari and Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif extended warm greetings to the Christian community, acknowledging its vital role in Pakistan’s progress and soci...

item.title: US-Iran tensions escalate amid threats and military actions in the Middle East
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East have sharply escalated following a series of military operations and aggressive statements from US President Donald Trump. The US and Israel conducted airstrikes in Iran an...
